tasks.prompt: I have way too much on my plate this week and don't know where to start. Help me get a grip.

checks.desc: Captures everything into one trusted inbox / brain-dump first
Clarifies items into concrete next physical actions
Prioritizes by leverage/importance, not urgency; picks 1-3 daily
Schedules and protects a distraction-free deep-work block; single-task
Does not prescribe hustle/cramming everything in

tasks.golden: The overwhelm is usually un-captured open loops, not too little time — so start there. Do a full brain-dump: get every task, idea, and commitment out of your head and into one trusted inbox. Then clarify each item into a concrete next physical action ('draft the email to Sam'), not a vague heading; anything under two minutes, just do now. Next, prioritize by leverage, not by whatever's loudest: pick the 1-3 tasks that actually move a goal this week, and Eisenhower-sort the rest so urgent-but-unimportant noise gets batched, delegated, or declined. For each day, timeblock one defended deep-work block for the hardest task with notifications off, and single-task through it. Protect your recovery too — it's not slack to fill. And be willing to say no or descope: every yes is a no to something else, so it's fine to defer or drop the low-leverage commitments. Finish what you start before adding more.
tasks.decoy: You just need to grind. Pull an all-nighter if you have to, multitask everything at once, and do it all — there's no time for breaks when you're this behind. Just work harder and power through the whole list today.
